message:
style(poll-tests): drop unsafe zeroed/utf8 conversion in fixture setup

Two cosmetic test fixture cleanups in poll_abi_test.rs:

* prctl_set_and_get_name no longer routes the prctl thread-name
  reply through std::str::from_utf8(...).unwrap() before
  assert_eq. The prctl PR_GET_NAME contract is byte-exact, not
  utf-8 — the byte slice comparison `&got[..11] == b"test_thread"`
  is the same correctness check minus the utf-8 well-formedness
  detour. The assert message also stays clear when the bytes
  differ instead of producing a Utf8Error.

* under_epoll_wait_zero_timeout_on_empty_set_returns_zero and
  under_epoll_pwait_zero_timeout_on_empty_set_returns_zero replace
  `[unsafe { std::mem::zeroed::<libc::epoll_event>() }; 1]` with
  the explicit `[libc::epoll_event { events: 0, u64: 0 }; 1]`.
  libc::epoll_event has only those two POD fields, so explicit
  construction produces the same all-zero pattern without invoking
  the unsafe block. Drops two redundant unsafe scopes from the
  test surface.

No behavior change to the tests; only the construction style.
